title: EndpointType uses Other(String) with hand-rolled serde to preserve unknown URIs for round-trip
description: EndpointType::Other(String) carries the original wire URI string so clients can forward or log endpoint capabilities they do not understand. The hand-rolled Serialize/Deserialize impls (via as_uri()/from_uri()) are required because #[serde(other)] on a unit variant erases the original value — deserializing 'urn:example:custom' would produce Other but as_uri() would have no string to return. A future contributor might suggest simplifying to #[serde(rename_all)] plus #[serde(other)] like the other enums in this file. That would be a regression: any unknown endpoint type URI would be silently discarded at deserialization, breaking round-trip for clients that relay or display endpoints they do not recognize. The same pattern is applied consistently to BodyType for the same reason.
